new_value: Completed via concurrent audit regression test

old_value: {"id":"gog-lite-j3j","title":"Add happy-path tests for primary user workflows","description":"Add command-level happy-path coverage for the primary user journeys highlighted in the README, including the two-step auth flow, Gmail draft save, Calendar create, and Docs write or export flows. The goal is to protect the main product experience rather than only the underlying helper logic.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"azurite0107@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T03:13:45Z","created_by":"Takashi Morikubo","updated_at":"2026-03-06T03:13:45Z"}

{"id":"gog-lite-j0m","title":"Cover symlink target escapes in docs export writes","description":"Add regression tests for writeFileAtomically and docs export output when the final target path is a symlink and when a parent directory is a symlink. Cover both allowed-output-dir and unrestricted modes, and if the current behavior is still vulnerable, encode that gap explicitly so a later fix has a failing regression to close.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"azurite0107@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T03:11:14Z","created_by":"Takashi Morikubo","updated_at":"2026-03-06T03:11:14Z"}

{"id":"gog-lite-z3c","title":"Make policy updates concurrency-safe","description":"Protect policy.json read-modify-write flows with locking so concurrent updates such as auth emergency-revoke cannot drop blocked accounts or approval requirements. Preserve current policy semantics and add regression tests for concurrent updates.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"azurite0107@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T03:05:03Z","created_by":"Takashi Morikubo","updated_at":"2026-03-06T03:05:03Z"}

{"id":"gog-lite-5cc","title":"Remove implicit dry_run=false from non-dry-run success responses","description":"Claude review on diff through commit 2d14cc7 flagged that augmentSuccessPayload currently injects dry_run=false into non-dry-run success responses when action is inferred. Revisit this to preserve the existing JSON contract, keep additive metadata coherent across services, and add regression tests that assert non-dry-run outputs do not grow misleading dry_run fields unless explicitly intended.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"azurite0107@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:58:48Z","created_by":"Takashi Morikubo","updated_at":"2026-03-06T07:58:48Z"}

{"id":"gog-lite-rw6","title":"Make success metadata inference deterministic","description":"Claude review on diff through commit 2d14cc7 found that inferResourceType relies on Go map iteration order, so payloads with multiple matching keys can emit non-deterministic resource_type values. Replace map iteration with deterministic precedence, remove dead inferAction branches, and add regression tests that prove stable output for overlapping-key payloads.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"azurite0107@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:58:48Z","created_by":"Takashi Morikubo","updated_at":"2026-03-06T07:58:48Z"}

{"id":"gog-lite-kry","title":"Strengthen JSON contract tests for public CLI responses","description":"Strengthen regression tests for the public JSON contract by asserting representative success and error payload shapes, not just individual fields like code or dry_run. Focus on top-level keys and compatibility-sensitive structure for the commands users and agents are most likely to depend on.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"azurite0107@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T03:13:45Z","created_by":"Takashi Morikubo","updated_at":"2026-03-06T03:13:45Z"}

{"id":"gog-lite-9m7","title":"Strengthen failure assertions for concurrent safety controls","description":"Strengthen the concurrent tests around approval tokens and rate limits so they assert failure behavior as well as success counts. Verify losing approval-token consumers fail with an already-used style error and the token file ends in Used=true, and verify rate-limit losers return the expected rate-limit error while the persisted state stops at the configured count.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"azurite0107@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T03:11:14Z","created_by":"Takashi Morikubo","updated_at":"2026-03-06T03:11:14Z"}

{"id":"gog-lite-1gy","title":"Improve dry-run predictability for write commands","description":"Add dry-run metadata such as requires_confirmation, requires_approval_token, would_call_api, and validation_passed so write commands are easier for agents to reason about before execution. Preserve the current dry_run response shape and add regression tests for representative write paths.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"azurite0107@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T03:07:38Z","created_by":"Takashi Morikubo","updated_at":"2026-03-06T03:07:38Z"}

{"id":"gog-lite-65v","title":"Reduce approval token exposure in CLI output","description":"Rework auth approval-token so the issued token is less likely to leak via stdout logs or agent transcripts. If the JSON contract must change, document the compatibility approach explicitly and preserve dry-run plus audit behavior.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"azurite0107@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T03:05:06Z","created_by":"Takashi Morikubo","updated_at":"2026-03-06T03:05:06Z"}

{"id":"gog-lite-kg5","title":"Add normalized metadata around raw Google API payloads","description":"Add stable normalized metadata such as resource_type, id, and account around commands that currently return raw Google API payloads, including gmail get and calendar get. Preserve the current raw payload shape for backward compatibility and add tests proving the normalized layer is additive.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"azurite0107@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T03:07:38Z","created_by":"Takashi Morikubo","updated_at":"2026-03-06T03:07:38Z"}

{"id":"gog-lite-j01","title":"Block symlink targets in docs export output","description":"Reject symlink-based output escapes in writeFileAtomically and docs export output handling, including symlinked target files and parent directories. Preserve current allowed-output-dir behavior and add regression tests covering both restricted and unrestricted output modes.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"azurite0107@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T03:05:09Z","created_by":"Takashi Morikubo","updated_at":"2026-03-06T03:05:09Z"}
